text: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy S twenty-six series at Unpacked in San Francisco, calling it the most intuitive AI phone yet.
Tom's Guide highlights an agentic AI that handles scheduling, summarizing, and research directly from conversations.
The Ultra features a two hundred megapixel camera and a breakthrough privacy display that blocks side views.
TechCrunch says the Ultra is zero point three millimeters thinner yet more powerful than its predecessor.
Engadget calls the lineup Samsung's strongest answer to Apple Intelligence.
Phones are evolving from tools you operate to assistants that act independently on your behalf.
This raises critical questions about privacy and how much control we are willing to hand over to machines.
